item.title: How much does a new HVAC system really cost in 2026?
item.description: HVAC costs have been surging recently, but some homeowners are paying far more than necessary for their systems.

item.title: Trump holds Cabinet meeting as negotiations continue to end Iran war
item.description: President Trump held a Cabinet meeting at the White House after moving it from Camp David due to weather.

item.title: Iran "negotiating on fumes," Trump says, as Israel ramps up war in Lebanon
item.description: As the U.S. and Iran keep up the bluster but appear ready to de-escalate, Israel expands deadly attacks on the complex war's other front in Lebanon.

item.title: Zelenskyy asks Trump for more Patriot missiles in war against Russia
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy sent a letter to President Trump over Memorial Day weekend asking for more Patriot missiles from the United States. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman has more.

item.title: Watch: Trump takes questions, says no one will control Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump took questions on a number of topics Wednesday at the White House during a Cabinet meeting. Following Trump's remarks, Natalie Brand provided more context.

item.title: Talarico: Paxton "clipping my cringey comments to distract from his career of corruption"
item.description: Texas state Rep. James Talarico, the Democratic nominee for U.S. Senate in the Lone Star State, concedes some past statements during debates over transgender policy "missed the mark."

item.title: U.S. setting up Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya, officials say
item.description: The U.S. is in the process of setting up a quarantine facility in Kenya for Americans who were exposed to Ebola​ or infected with the deadly illness.

item.title: Full interview: Democratic Texas Senate nominee James Talarico
item.description: State Rep. James Talarico, the Democratic candidate for Texas Senate, speaks with CBS News' Ed O'Keefe for his first network interview since state Attorney General Ken Paxton defeated incumbent John Cornyn in the GOP runoff election. Talarico called Paxton the "most corrupt politician in America" and slammed him for giving out "Epstein-style sweetheart deals to pedophiles."

item.title: Zelenskyy pushes U.S. for more Patriot missiles, warning of Russian attacks
item.description: President Volodymyr Zelenskyy urged the U.S. to expand deliveries of Patriot missile systems, warning that Russia is preparing a broader campaign of ballistic missile attacks.

item.title: TV bailiff accused of murder sobs, "I live with it every day"
item.description: Renard Spivey was found not guilty of his wife's murder, but he says he still can't sleep at night.

item.title: TV bailiff accused in wife's shooting death: "I didn't pull the trigger"
item.description: Renard Spivey says he was trying to protect himself when he says his wife Patricia confronted him at gunpoint in their Houston home.

item.title: Sneak peek: Deputy Spivey on Trial
item.description: ENCORE: A TV bailiff accused of murdering his wife is determined to prove his innocence. "48 Hours" contributor Natalie Morales has his first interview Saturday, May 30 at 10/9c* on CBS and streaming on Paramount+. (Airs following the WNBA on CBS)

item.title: How much interest can an 18-month CD account earn now?
item.description: An 18-month CD will protect your money well into 2027 while allowing you to earn a substantial return in the interim.

item.title: Alabama asks Supreme Court to allow congressional map found racially discriminatory
item.description: Alabama Republicans asked the Supreme Court to allow the state to use a congressional map that would give the GOP an edge in the midterm elections.

item.title: Trump insists Iran negotiations are progressing during Cabinet meeting
item.description: President Trump spoke to reporters during his Cabinet meeting on Wednesday at the White House, where he insisted negotiations with Iran are progressing and touted U.S. border control policies. CBS News' Nancy Cordes has more.

item.title: 5 of 7 people trapped in flooded Laos cave for more than a week found alive
item.description: Five villagers stuck in a flooded cave in central Laos for more than a week were found alive, rescuers said Wednesday, but two others are missing.

item.title: Teachers union president calls for AI and other technology limits in schools
item.description: Randi Weingarten, the president of the American Federation of Teachers, is calling for limits on technology in schools as part of her 10-point action plan to set public schools up for success. CBS News' Katrina Kaufman has more.

item.title: James Talarico responds to GOP attacks over past remark that God is "non-binary"
item.description: Texas state Rep. and Democratic Senate nominee James Talarico responded to Republican attacks over his past remark that God is "non-binary." "There are some statements that I've made that I certainly regret," He told CBS News' Ed O'Keefe, but accused his GOP opponent Ken Paxton of "intentionally clipping my cringey comments to distract from his career of corruption."

item.title: James Talarico slams Ken Paxton for giving "Epstein-style sweetheart deals to pedophiles"
item.description: Texas state Sen. James Talarico, the Democratic nominee for Texas Senate, pushed back on his GOP opponent Ken Paxton calling him "Tala-freak-o." "If Ken Paxton is worried about freaks, he should stop giving Epstein-style sweetheart deals to pedophiles," Talarico told CBS News' Ed O'Keefe in his first network TV interview since Paxton won the GOP Texas Senate runoff election.

item.title: Is a shifting tide behind a Russian warning to foreigners in Ukraine?
item.description: As Russia hammers Ukraine with bombs and warns foreigners to flee its capital, analysts think it may reflect a war "shifting in favor of Ukrainian forces."

item.title: Former U.S. Men's National Team goalkeeper weighs in on 2026 World Cup roster
item.description: Tony Meola, a former professional goalkeeper who represented the U.S. in the 1990, 1994, and 2002 World Cups, joins CBS News 24/7 to weigh in on this year's team roster.

item.title: Bondi undergoing treatment for thyroid cancer after recent diagnosis
item.description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi is undergoing treatment for thyroid cancer, she told CBS News.

item.title: Iran releases video of apparent U.S. drone wreck as tensions rise again in Middle East
item.description: Iran is claiming it shot down a U.S. drone as the world awaits news of progress in peace talks. CBS News' Charlie D'Agata reports.

item.title: Key U.S. players to watch for the 2026 World Cup
item.description: The U.S. Men's National Team announced its roster for the 2026 FIFA World Cup on Tuesday. CBS News' Nicole Valdes breaks it down.

item.title: Millions attend Hajj rituals for Eid al-Adha celebrations as Middle East conflict continues
item.description: Millions of pilgrims are attending the Hajj rituals for the Eid al-Adha celebrations despite the ongoing war in the Middle East and other concerns. CBS News' Courtney Kealy reports.

item.title: What happens to a wage garnishment if you change jobs?
item.description: Switching jobs won't erase a wage garnishment, but it could have an impact on when and how creditors can collect.

item.title: Latino voters turning away from Trump, but warnings remain for Dems, poll says
item.description: Hispanic voters are heading into the midterms feeling economically squeezed and increasingly skeptical of the direction of the country — but Democrats are not necessarily running away with their support, a new poll by UnidosUS finds.

item.title: Minneapolis Police Chief Brian O'Hara resigns after internal probe
item.description: Mayor Jacob Frey announced O'Hara's resignation late Tuesday after an investigation found he deleted a contact from his city-issued phone to hide his connection to an employee during a prior investigation.

item.title: Meet the 2026 World Cup U.S. Men's National Team
item.description: The squad was announced during an event in New York City on Tuesday after U.S. Men's National Team manager Mauricio Pochettino spent months evaluating players to finalize the roster.

item.title: "48 Hours" probes murder of Aspen legend Nancy Pfister
item.description: An Aspen "true original" is found brutally slain in her mountain home. She had enemies -- who wanted her dead?
